candidates.title: Metro Vancouver workers responsible for cleaning, maintaining parks and trails walk off the job
candidates.description: Over 100 workers with Metro Vancouver's regional parks walked off the job on Sunday, with the union citing member frustration over lack of movement in talks with the district.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 2 people pulled from Montreal's Lachine canal overnight in critical condition
candidates.description: Rescue teams spent hours looking for two individuals in Montreal's Lachine Canal in the overnight hours of Saturday to Sunday. They were both taken to hospital in critical condition.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Proposed class actions target City of Montreal, West Island suburbs, after floods
candidates.description: Two class-action lawsuits have been filed in Quebec Superior Court after heavy rains on June 20 led to flooding in parts of Montreal's West Island. The proposed lawsuits allege the municipalities didn't do enough to mitigate known flood risks.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Winnipeg quarterback Zach Collaros 'bummed' to miss Travis Kelce's wedding to Taylor Swift over game schedule
candidates.description: Blue Bombers quarterback Zach Collaros said he was "bummed" to miss out on seeing his longtime friend and NFLer Travis Kelce marry pop icon Taylor Swift at a star-studded wedding at Madison Square Garden in New York City on Friday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Brandon, Man., declares state of emergency as overflowing Assiniboine River forecast to continue to rise
candidates.description: The City of Brandon has declared a state of emergency, with the Assiniboine River spilling its banks days ahead of a forecast flood peak on July 12.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Overdose calls to paramedics on the rise in several Ontario cities, CBC analysis shows
candidates.description: Non-fatal opioid overdose calls are on the rise in four Ontario cities — nearly tripling in Hamilton in the first five months of 2026. Researchers and social workers suggest the toxic drug supply and the closure of supervised consumption sites in the past two years may be factors.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ontario transit agency issued parking ticket for a car stolen from a train station. Its owner is still fighting it
candidates.description: When Nancy Griffin parked her vehicle at the Oshawa GO station on March 25, she expected a routine trip to and from Toronto. Instead, she says she found herself at the centre of a months-long ordeal involving a stolen vehicle, confusing bureaucracy, and a parking ticket for a car she no longer possessed.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Ottawa councillors, MPPs call for help from province after Canada Day deluge
candidates.description: Several Ottawa city councillors and MPPs have signed an open letter calling on the province to make disaster relief funding available for residents facing financial burdens after last week’s storm.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Throwback: When Pokémon GO took over Canada’s capital
candidates.description: We're coming up on the 10-year anniversary of when Pokémon GO, the augmented reality app, first arrived in Canada. The game kicked off a wave of fandom among kids and adults, including those on Parliament Hill.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Toronto had little economic gain during World Cup's first 2 weeks in city, data shows
candidates.description: Data collected between June 12 and 26 — the first two weeks of the World Cup in Toronto —showed that debit and credit card spending at restaurants and bars in the city rose by just three per cent compared with the same time last year.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: No houses lost in Fort Simpson, N.W.T., wildfire
candidates.description: Some outbuildings have been damaged confirm officials on Saturday morning, with a challenging day ahead as fire grows in the south and continues to burn in residential areas
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: The test of time: Canada’s only watchmaking school still ticking after 80 years
candidates.description: The École national d’horlogerie was founded after WW II as a way to help veterans reintegrate into society. Today, it’s about training professional watchmakers for the global market.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Warm rivers raise concern for salmon in B.C.'s Interior
candidates.description: Warm rivers and streams are becoming a growing concern for salmon in B.C.’s Interior with another low sockeye return anticipated in Okanagan waters this fall.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Kashechewan evacuees in Niagara Falls, Ont., get apology from mayor over homelessness comments
candidates.description: Evacuees from Kashechewan First Nation who have been living in Niagara Falls, Ont., for the last six months marched in sweltering heat through the city's downtown Friday to demand an apology over recent comments made by the city's former chief administrative officer and by the local mayor.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Heartbroken girlfriend of B.C. captain tells of close call days before deadly sinking
candidates.description: The heartbroken girlfriend of a young charter boat captain feared drowned in a deadly sinking in B.C. waters last Sunday says she was aboard the vessel two days earlier when a broken door swung open and water flooded in.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Son who succeeded slain Iranian leader Khamenei absent from funeral as 3 other sons appear
candidates.description: The son who succeeded Iran's slain leader wasn't at the funeral for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and four other family members on Sunday, while three other sons showed up and prayed beside the coffins.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Hundreds of firefighters battle wildfire in Portugal, Greek authorities warn of toxic smoke
candidates.description: Hundreds of firefighters battled wildfires in Portugal and Greece on Sunday, with Spain and Italy sending reinforcements to help contain a massive blaze that's been burning for more than three days.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: FIFA suspends U.S. forward's 1-game ban, allowing him to play against Belgium in Round of 16
candidates.description: Folarin Balogun's one-game ban was surprisingly suspended by FIFA, clearing the American forward to play in the team's World Cup round of 16 match against Belgium, a decision that was praised by U.S. President Donald Trump and promoted outrage from the Red Devils.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Djokovic breaks Federer's Wimbledon record with 106th match win to reach quarterfinals
candidates.description: Novak Djokovic screamed out his frustrations at times before overcoming a surprisingly tough challenge from 132nd-ranked qualifier Roman Safiullin on Sunday to break one of Roger Federer's Wimbledon records.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Trump offers to help Putin find deal with Ukraine, U.S. president also speaks with Zelenskyy
candidates.description: U.S. President Donald Trump, speaking to Russian leader Vladimir Putin by telephone ‌for nearly 90 minutes, offered to help find a solution to the Ukraine war, Kremlin aide Yuri Ushakov said in comments made public early on Sunday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Pope Leo draws hard line on breakaway traditionalists, but it could get messy
candidates.description: As the Vatican excommunicated four bishops of the ultra-traditionalist Society of Saint Pius X this week, notice was also served that the society’s priests and lay Catholics who formally adhere to it are also considered cut off from the Roman Catholic Church — a move that raises the spiritual stakes for thousands of followers worldwide.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: St. Petersburg oil terminal hit as Ukraine presses long-range drone campaign
candidates.description: A Ukrainian drone attack struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg on Saturday, Russian officials said, as Kyiv pressed on with the bombardment of Russia’s oil infrastructure.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Venezuelan doctors return home to give aid amid quake devastation
candidates.description: Marianyi Bastidas is one of several doctors and specialists who left their birth country of Venezuela years ago, but returned to help after the disastrous twin earthquakes in late June.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Crowds mourn Ayatollah Ali Khamenei as Iran begins days-long funeral ceremonies
candidates.description: Hundreds of thousands of mourners began a days-long funeral on Saturday for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s late supreme leader, beating their chests in sorrow before the glass case containing his flag-draped coffin in Tehran and calling for revenge against Israel and the United States.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Alberta’s pipeline pitch is heavy on public, light on private investment — so who benefits?
candidates.description: Energy experts and economists say Alberta’s public-private pitch may be the only way to get a pipelines done in today's context, but if there are benefits, they could go beyond just the oil and gas industry.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Canada wants to build up to 10 new nuclear plants. Will our pension funds pay for them?
candidates.description: Canada's ambitious new nuclear strategy has called for private investment to help expand the industry. Given that nuclear power plants are notoriously expensive to build, the government will have to heavily de-risk the ventures to attract large investors, like pension funds.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
candidates.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Lyse Doucet on emotion and politics at Tehran funeral of former supreme leader
candidates.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Tehran where funeral events are taking place in honour of Iran's former leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: From 'a beautiful night' to 'world's greatest hangover', guests react to Taylor and Travis's wedding
candidates.description: Some of the famous guests have been posting on social media about the big day in New York.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Moment of destiny for France's Le Pen in verdict to decide her future in presidential race
candidates.description: Far-right leader Marine Le Pen is appealing against a verdict which found her guilty of misusing EU funds.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Australia probes mystery space balls that washed up on beach
candidates.description: Officials are searching for the origins of six pieces of space debris discovered on Forrest Beach in Queensland.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Evacuations in Guam as super typhoon Bavi approaches
candidates.description: The storm is forecast to bring winds in excess of 160mph and waves nearly 11m high when it makes landfall on Monday.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Eight rescued from seaplane that made 'hard landing' in Manhattan river
candidates.description: Eight people were removed from the Kodiak 100 seaplane by fire department units and two had minor injures.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump thanks Fifa for spending USA striker's ban
candidates.description: Folarin Balogun is available to play for the United States in their World Cup last-16 tie against Belgium after his one-game ban is suspended by Fifa.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: How Iran's new regime is very different to what came before
candidates.description: Khamenei's funeral is another reminder of the change Iran has seen, but what does its new leadership want?
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Cowboys, fighter jets and US Border Patrol - inside Trump's big recruitment drive
candidates.description: Space Force and Customs and Border Patrol are using a rodeo as a way to drive up recruitment numbers.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: France survive Paraguay's 'disgraceful' and 'embarrassing' dark arts
candidates.description: After dismantling all before them at this World Cup, France had to find a new way to win against a Paraguay side whose use of the dark arts angered many of those watching.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Dior dress, Adam Sandler and a man of honour: What we know about Taylor and Travis's wedding
candidates.description: The couple tied the knot in New York on Friday, after months of speculation about when, where and how it would happen.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Hotter and hotter and hotter' - Europe's new climate in seven charts
candidates.description: Temperature records were smashed in June - and scientists warn this is a sign of things to come.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Emotion and politics merge in Tehran at funeral of former supreme leader
candidates.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent Lyse Doucet is in Tehran, where funeral events are taking place in honour of Iran's former leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Scorching heat and delays: How Washington DC marked America 250
candidates.description: The United States of America marked its 250th birthday with fireworks, flyovers, some intense weather across the country.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Funeral of Iran's former supreme leader 'intensely political moment'
candidates.description: Authorities expect up to 20 million people to attend ceremonies across Iran and Iraq over the coming days.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: BBC in Tehran as mourners gather for former supreme leader's funeral
candidates.description: Iranian authorities say they expect millions of people to take part in funeral commemorations for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce wed in NYC - here's how the day unfolded
candidates.description: The couple tied the knot in a star-studded ceremony officiated by comedian Adam Sandler at Madison Square Garden.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Folarin Balogun's ban is lifted by FIFA, allowing U.S. striker to play Belgium
candidates.description: FIFA's Disciplinary Committee said it would suspend Balogun's one-game red card ban for a probationary period of one year. The Royal Belgian Football Federation said it was "astonished."
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Guam and surrounding Pacific islands brace for impact of Super Typhoon Bavi
candidates.description: People in the Northern Mariana Islands – remote U.S. territories in the Pacific Ocean – are preparing for Super Typhoon Bavi, which experts say could bring winds of over 180 miles per hour.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Pastor freed from prison in China weeks after Trump requested his release
candidates.description: A pastor of a prominent underground church who was detained in China in October has been released, less than two months after U.S. President Donald Trump brought up his case when meeting Chinese leader Xi Jinping.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: A new phase in the war in Ukraine
candidates.description: Ukraine says it can now hit military and energy targets deep inside Russia. Former ambassador Daniel Fried explains why he thinks Russia is starting to lose its strategic advantage.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Pope Leo visits Lampedusa to spotlight missing migrants
candidates.description: Pope Leo XIV will spend July 4th in Lampedusa, Italy, one of Europe's busiest migrant landing points. He will pray with migrants and honor those who died trying to cross the Mediterranean to Europe.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Iran begins week of funeral celebrations for Khamenei
candidates.description: Foreign dignitaries are gathering in Iran for a week of funeral ceremonies for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, more than four months after he was killed in U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: China's military promotes 2 new generals after anti-corruption purge thins ranks
candidates.description: The shake-up is believed to be an effort to ensure the military's loyalty to the Communist Party and its leader, Xi Jinping.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Cape Verde's historic World Cup ride ends after pushing Argentina to the brink
candidates.description: Cape Verde didn't win a match at the World Cup, and somehow, that didn't seem to matter. The African team's debut on this stage was unforgettable.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Dayslong funeral for slain Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei begins in Tehran
candidates.description: Khamenei, who ruled Iran for more than three decades, was killed in an airstrike on Feb. 28 at the start of the U.S.-Israeli war against Iran.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Iran's control of the Strait of Hormuz remains a powerful bargaining chip
candidates.description: Despite efforts by U.S. negotiators, Iran says it wants to charge a toll for ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz. It's yet another unresolved issue of the U.S.-Iran war.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Egg producers settle with DOJ, states over price-fixing complaint
candidates.description: Seventeen states and the Department of Justice have settled a case of alleged price-fixing by major egg producers. Egg prices soared in recent years but have since fallen sharply.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Construction is a man's game. These women are demolishing the barriers
candidates.description: Women make up a miniscule 3% of construction workers in Kenya. But a nonprofit group is offering them a way to get into the game ... and to get ahead.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Move over Chutes & Ladders: Schisto & Ladders has educational value plus worms
candidates.description: Spread by parasitic worms that can infect via a splash of water, schistosomiasis causes debilitating symptoms. It hits kids hard. A board game teaches how to prevent it.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: CDC emails reveal challenges facing its next director
candidates.description: Emails from CDC officials released by Sen. Bernie Sanders reveal agency leaders dealing with political pressures during the Trump era and highlight challenges for the next director.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 5 at 3:34PM CDT by NWS Jackson MS
candidates.description: At 334 PM CDT, Doppler radar was tracking a cluster of strong thunderstorms over West Hattiesburg, moving east at 15 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 40 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Hattiesburg, Petal, West Hattiesburg, Oak Grove, Rawls Springs, Moselle, Eastabuchie, Sunrise, and Macedonia.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Warning issued July 5 at 3:34PM CDT until July 6 at 8:20PM CDT by NWS Chicago IL
candidates.description: ...Forecast flooding changed to Minor severity and increased in duration for the following rivers in Indiana...Illinois... Little Calumet River at Munster (Hohman Avenue) affecting Cook and Lake IN Counties. ...The Flood Warning is extended for the following rivers in Illinois... Thorn Creek at Thornton affecting Cook County. For the Little Calumet River...including Munster (Hohman Avenue)... Minor flooding is forecast. * WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and minor flooding is forecast. * WHERE...Thorn Creek from Near Glenwood downstream to confluence with Little Calumet River, including the Thornton gauge. * WHEN...Until tomorrow evening. * IMPACTS...At 13.0 feet, Parking lots threatened at Glenwood Academy. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 3:15 PM CDT Sunday the stage was 12.5 feet. - Recent Activity...The maximum river stage in the 24 hours ending at 3:15 PM CDT Sunday was 12.5 feet. - Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage tomorrow afternoon. - Flood stage is 9.0 feet. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Warning issued July 5 at 3:34PM CDT until July 6 at 1:00PM CDT by NWS Chicago IL
candidates.description: ...Forecast flooding changed to Minor severity and increased in duration for the following rivers in Indiana...Illinois... Little Calumet River at Munster (Hohman Avenue) affecting Cook and Lake IN Counties. ...The Flood Warning is extended for the following rivers in Illinois... Thorn Creek at Thornton affecting Cook County. For the Little Calumet River...including Munster (Hohman Avenue)... Minor flooding is forecast. * WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and minor flooding is forecast. * WHERE...Little Calumet River from IN-912 in Hammond downstream to confluence with Thorn Creek in South Holland, including the Munster (Hohman Avenue) gauge. * WHEN...Until early tomorrow afternoon. * IMPACTS...At 12.5 feet, Most areas outside of the levee-protected area are inundated in Hammond and Munster. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 3:00 PM CDT Sunday the stage was 12.2 feet. - Recent Activity...The maximum river stage in the 24 hours ending at 3:00 PM CDT Sunday was 12.6 feet. - Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage late tonight or tomorrow morning. - Flood stage is 12.0 feet. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Flood Advisory issued July 5 at 4:34PM EDT until July 5 at 7:30PM EDT by NWS Jacksonville FL
candidates.description: * WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected. * WHERE...Central Glynn County in southeastern Georgia... * WHEN...Until 730 PM EDT. * IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS... - At 434 PM EDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain have fallen. - Additional rainfall amounts up to 2 inches are expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding. - Some locations that will experience flooding include... Dock Junction, Country Club Estates and Boys Estate. - http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 5 at 3:34PM CDT until July 5 at 4:30PM CDT by NWS Lubbock TX
candidates.description: SVRLUB The National Weather Service in Lubbock Texas has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Southern Cottle County in northwestern Texas... * Until 430 PM CDT. * At 334 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located 4 miles west of Paducah, and is nearly stationary. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Paducah, Delwin, Chalk, Swearingen, and Hackberry.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 5 at 4:33PM EDT until July 5 at 5:30PM EDT by NWS State College PA
candidates.description: SVRCTP The National Weather Service in State College PA has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... South central Centre County in central Pennsylvania... Northeastern Huntingdon County in central Pennsylvania... Northwestern Mifflin County in central Pennsylvania... * Until 530 PM EDT. * At 432 PM EDT, severe thunderstorms were located along a line extending from Stormstown to Houserville to Reedsville, moving north at 15 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... State College, Bellefonte, Park Forest Village, Boalsburg, Pleasant Gap, Stormstown, Lemont, Zion, Belleville, Houserville, Pine Grove Mills, and Milroy. For those driving on Interstate 80, this includes areas between the Snow Shoe and Lamar exits, specifically from mile markers 157 to 161. This includes Interstate 99 from mile markers 64 to 85.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Marine Warning issued July 5 at 3:33PM CDT until July 5 at 4:45PM CDT by NWS New Orleans LA
candidates.description: For the following areas... Lake Borgne... Lake Maurepas... Lake Pontchartrain... At 333 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm capable of producing waterspouts was located near Lake Pontchartrain/Lake Maurepas, moving southeast at 25 knots. HAZARD...Waterspouts, wind gusts to 40 knots, and small hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Waterspouts can quickly form and capsize boats...damage vessels and oil rigs...and create suddenly higher waves. Small craft could be damaged in briefly higher winds and suddenly higher waves. Locations impacted include... Lake Pontchartrain/Lake Maurepas, Orleans Marina, Rigolets, Lake Maurepas, The Mid Point Of The Causeway Bridge, Eden Isle, Kenner, and Lacombe.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Special Weather Statement issued July 5 at 4:33PM EDT by NWS Charleston SC
candidates.description: At 433 PM EDT, Doppler radar was tracking a line of strong thunderstorms extending from near Tillman to over Eden, moving east at 10 mph. HAZARD...Wind gusts of 50 to 55 mph. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around unsecured objects. Locations impacted include... Pooler, Springfield, Godley Station, Rincon, Port Wentworth, Stillwell, Ebenezer, Purysburgh, Pineora and Marlow.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 5 at 4:33PM EDT until July 5 at 5:00PM EDT by NWS Wakefield VA
candidates.description: At 433 PM EDT, severe thunderstorms were located along a line extending from near Hadensville to near Bumpass, moving north at 10 to 15 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to trees and powerlines. Locations impacted include... Mineral, Gum Spring, Bumpass, Orchid, Pendleton, Beaverdam, Fredericks Hall, Locust Creek, Apple Grove, and Cuckoo.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued July 5 at 3:32PM CDT until July 5 at 4:15PM CDT by NWS Lubbock TX
candidates.description: At 331 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located 8 miles north of Afton, or 9 miles south of Matador, moving south at 15 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. Locations impacted include... Afton, Mcadoo, Dumont, and Roaring Springs.
candidates.author: National Weather Service
